texto: But what I'm very interested in, whether it's writing, whether it's hosting a show, whether it's cooking food, I'm just into the discussions of identity, culture and the politics of culture.

texto: I wanted to inspire people not to work under a bamboo ceiling. Whatever you are - yellow, black, white, brown - you don't have to allow your skin to define who you are or how you operate your business. There's not one face to anything.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Eddie Huang sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “I wanted to inspire people not to work under a bamboo ceiling. Whatever you are - yellow, black, …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, work, inspire, útil em momentos de inspiracao e trabalho.

texto: People ask me what my greatest strengths are and I say perspective. The best way to get that is to meet people that are polar opposites; you learn the most from them. There are pieces of you that are inherently yours, but everything else is a collection of the things you’ve seen and the people you’ve met.

texto: No matter how talented Richie was or how much shit he talked about what he could do, deep down he just didn't believe it'd happen. He was living day to day: you talk about your dreams, you boast about your talent, and you cop the Foamposites the day they come out because life is simply a collection of small victories. I didn't want to go out like that.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Eddie Huang: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “No matter how talented Richie was or how much shit he talked about what he could do, deep down he…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, dreams, small-victories pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de small-victories.

texto: Take the things from America that speak to you, that excite you, that inspire you, and be the Americans we all want to know; then cook it up and sell it back to them for $28.99. Cue Funk Flex to drop bombs on this. All my peoples from the boat, let 'em know: WEOUTCHEA.

texto: We play into the definitions and stereotypes others impose on us and accept the model-minority myth, thinking it's positive, but it's a trap just like any stereotype. They put a piece of model-minority cheese between the metal jaws of their mousetrap, but we're lactose intolerant anyway! We can't even eat the cheese.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Eddie Huang: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “We play into the definitions and stereotypes others impose on us and accept the model-minority my…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, asian-american, race pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de race e stereotypes.
